subject: [PATCH 00/14] userfaultfd: working set tracking for VM guest memory

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (Meta)
reviews.summary: The reviewer provided a detailed explanation of the problem and approach taken in the patch series. They highlighted the benefits of using userfaultfd for working set tracking and described the new registration mode (RWP) and its functionality.

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: The reviewer asked for clarification on the interaction between RWP and existing modes, specifically how re-registering a range would affect per-PTE markers.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: The reviewer suggested that the patch series should be split into smaller, more manageable pieces to facilitate review and discussion.

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for tracking the working set of VM guest memory using userfaultfd, enabling a VMM to identify cold pages and evict them to tiered or remote storage. The approach involves introducing a new userfaultfd registration mode (RWP) that uses the same mechanism as existing modes but with additional functionality.
